member.office_address: 455 Russell Senate Office Building Washington DC 20510

form_analysis.form_header_text: FY 2027 Congressionally Directed Spending Request Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Office of U.S. Senator Tommy Tuberville. Senator Tuberville will accept congressionally directed spending (CDS) requests to support critical infrastructure, law enforcement, and job training and health care equipment needs in the state of Alabama this appropriations cycle. CDS requests submitted for other types of projects will not be considered. Only requests submitted by public entities - county and municipal governments, for example - and non-profit hospitals will be considered. Requests submitted by private entities will not be considered. A preference will be given to projects that have sought funding through the federal grant process in the past. A preference will be given to projects submitted by entities willing to provide a cost share for funds requested.

sections.description: Criteria for eligible entities and priority project types.

fields.label: Is the applicant a public entity or a non-profit hospital? Only public entities and non-profit hospitals are eligible to apply.
fields.rationale: This field requires submitter-specific information (the identity of the organization filing the form) which is not provided in the context. However, The Planetary Society is a 501(c)(3) non-profit, but it is not a hospital.

fields.label: Has the applicant sought funding for this specific project through the federal grant process in the past? If so, to which federal grant program did the applicant apply?
fields.rationale: This is a user-specific question regarding their individual application history which cannot be determined from the provided context.

fields.label: Is the applicant willing and able to share some of the cost of the project? If so, please list the cost share percentage.
fields.rationale: Cost-sharing capability is specific to the requesting institution and is not provided in the strategic context.

fields.label: If the project is related to road or bridge construction or improvement, is the project listed on the Alabama Department of Transportation's Statewide Transportation Plan or a related priorities list?
fields.rationale: This request is for NASA Science Mission Directorate funding and is not related to road or bridge construction.

sections.description: Details regarding the specific project, timeline, and jurisdiction.

fields.label: Project Name
fields.rationale: Matches the requested program name as specified in the directions and reference values.

fields.label: Amount of CDS Funding Requested
fields.rationale: Matches the exact funding ask amount provided in the reference values for NASA's Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: If there are project costs in addition to the CDS amount requested, have those funds been secured?
fields.rationale: This depends on the internal financial status of the applicant's broader project portfolio, which is not provided.

fields.label: Can the projected be completed in phases over multiple years?
fields.rationale: NASA Science Mission Directorate (SMD) programs are inherently multi-year endeavors, as evidenced by the provided spending context showing contract and grant periods extending through 2032 (e.g., University of Alabama in Huntsville cooperative agreement).

fields.label: Appropriations Subcommittee of Jurisdicton
fields.help_text: Select the subcommittee with jurisdiction over the funding request.
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies
Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
Labor, Health and Human Services, Education, and Related Agencies
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
Transportation, Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: The reference values explicitly state that the NASA Science Mission Directorate falls under the jurisdiction of the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies Subcommittee.

fields.label: Federal Department Associated with the Project (Example: Department of Transportation)
fields.rationale: Identifies the parent agency for the request.

fields.label: Federal Agency Associated with the Project (Example: Office of Infrastructure Finance and Innovation within USDOT)
fields.rationale: Identifies the specific directorate (agency subunit) for the programmatic request.

fields.label: Federal Account Associated with the Project (Example: RAISE Grant)
fields.rationale: The account name as listed in the reference values and the NASA budget structure.

fields.label: Project Description
fields.draft_value:
* Funding for NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) at $9 billion to execute critical planetary, Earth, heliophysics, and astrophysics missions.
* Supports deep space engineering and ground test operations vital to the Marshall Space Flight Center ecosystem.
* Enables continued development of missions prioritized by National Academies Decadal Surveys to maintain American scientific leadership.
fields.rationale: Description summarizes the $9 billion request for SMD and connects it to the Alabama-based Marshall Space Flight Center ecosystem and the Decadal Survey process mentioned in the advocacy context.

fields.label: Project Location
fields.rationale: NASA Science funding supports institutions across Alabama, including Huntsville (UAH, Amentum), Tuscaloosa (UA), and Auburn.

fields.label: Project Timeline
fields.rationale: This is an appropriations request for the upcoming federal fiscal year.

fields.label: Justification Project
fields.help_text: Provide justification for why this project should receive federal funding.
fields.draft_value:
* Sustains a vital high-tech workforce in Alabama, which saw $1.74 billion in NASA Science obligations between FY2023 and FY2025.
* Protects domestic manufacturing and engineering capabilities at firms like Amentum Technology ($1.79B award) and RSI-Quantitech JV ($193M award).
* Counteracts rapid Chinese expansion in space science to ensure the United States continues to set the norms for 21st-century exploration.
* Supports critical research at institutions like UA Huntsville, which manages over $260 million in recent NASA Science grants.
fields.rationale: The justification utilizes local spending data for Alabama, specifically citing large contract awards for Amentum and RSI-Quantitech, and grant totals for UA Huntsville to demonstrate district-level economic impact alongside strategic competition arguments.

fields.label: Does this project have broad community support? Please provide examples.
fields.draft_value: Yes. Support includes major Alabama institutions like The University of Alabama in Huntsville and partners like the American Astronomical Society and American Geophysical Union.
fields.rationale: Cites local grant recipients (UAH) and national allied organizations listed in the reference values.

fields.label: Has the applicant discussed this project with the state-level office of jurisdiction? (Example: Alabama Department of Transportation)
fields.rationale: As a federal programmatic request for NASA, there is typically no state-level office of jurisdiction like ALDOT for space science research.

sections.description: Information regarding the organization making the request and the primary point of contact.

fields.label: Name of Entity Submitting Request (Example: Wilcox County Commission)
Employer Identification Number (EIN) of Entity Submitting Request
Point of Contact at Entity Submitting Request (Example: County Commission Chairman)
Email Address for the Point of Contact
Cell Phone Number for the Point of Contact
Has the same projected been submitted to other offices within the Alabama delegation? If yes, please list the office.
fields.draft_value: Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading an existing multi-member House letter in support of this request. Request also being submitted to additional House offices.
fields.rationale: Request is being submitted to multiple offices; Bacon/Chu lead the House letter per reference values.

top_contracts.description: Engineering Services and Science Capability Augmentation (ESSCA)
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Securing critical engineering support for deep space missions.

top_contracts.description: Marshall engineering technicians and trades support (METTS III) covers a wide range of engineering technicians and trade skills to perform testing ground and space based research test operations data analysis instrumentation calibration
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Technician support ensuring successful ground and space test operations.

top_contracts.description: Program support services (CPSS) programmatic Subject Matter Expert (SME) idiq task order for Marshall Space Flight Center (MSFC).
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Securing expert technical guidance for Marshall Space Flight Center missions.

top_grants.recipient_name: The University of Alabama in Huntsville
top_grants.description: The National Space Science and Technology Center (NSSTC) is a research facility located in cramer hall on the campus of the university of alabama in
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Sustaining the National Space Science and Technology Center research facility.

top_grants.recipient_name: The University of Alabama in Huntsville
top_grants.description: This award is a 10-year renewal cooperative agreement with uah. This cooperative agreement is a continuation of the NASA/MSFC collaborative research efforts with uah at the National Space Science and Technology Center (NSSTC). The cooperative agreement is a collaborative effort that provides an environment for Space research  education  and development in support of NASA'S mission. The key focus areas to be included are Earth Science research and analysis  Earth Science applications research  heliophysics and Space weather  planetary Science  astrophysics and "other" (E.g. Biotechnology  materials Science  modeling & simulation  systems engineering and emerging disciplines).
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Cooperative agreement advancing Earth and space science research.

top_grants.description: The roses funding opportunity goals are:...execute a balanced science program based on discipline-specific guidance from the national academies of sciences  engineering  and medicine  administration priorities  and direction from congress....participate as a key partner and enabler in the agency'S exploration initiative  focusing on scientific research of  on  and from the moon  lunar orbit  Mars  and beyond....advance discovery in emerging fields by identifying and exploiting cross-disciplinary opportunities between traditional science disciplines...develop a directorate-wide  target-user focused approach to applied programs  including Earth science applications  space weather  planetary defense  and space situational awareness..
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Funding diverse research supporting Moon, Mars exploration, and planetary defense.

top_grants.description: 22-apra22-0124  large-scale aluminum metal matrix composite mirrors for cryogenic applications
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Manufacturing large composite mirrors for cryogenic space missions.

top_grants.recipient_name: The University of Alabama in Huntsville
top_grants.description: The science objectives of this investigation are to characterize  quantitatively  the magnetic reconnection rate in various plasma regimes at the reconnection sites  i.E.  Current sheets.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Measuring magnetic field snapping rates in space plasmas.

top_grants.recipient_name: The University of Alabama in Huntsville
top_grants.description: Science goals and objectives. The main goals of this project are: (I) investigation of the fundamental physics of how  and to what extent  dynamic small-scale magnetic flux ropes (SMFRS)  observed to occur in the vicinity of shocks driven by coronal
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Researching fundamental physics of small magnetic flux ropes near solar shocks.

top_grants.description: Understanding the radiation environment at Earth is critical due to both scientific interests and practical needs. However  the acceleration mechanism of Earth S energetic particles consisting of radiation belts is neither fully understood nor quantified because of 1) sparse in-situ observations between the altitude of ~5.8 re and geosynchronous orbit (GEO) and 2) difficulties to disentangle the effect of different acceleration mechanisms. This proposal aims to develop a miniaturized  high-energy-resolution relativistic electron telescope (HERT) that can be easily accommodated into future cubesat/smallsat missions to understand and quantify the effect of acceleration mechanism throughout the entire radiation belts to geo using a novel method of probing electron flux oscillations. Hert is a compact (<3U) telescope with the ability to provide high energy resolution (de/E<10%) measurements of 1   7 mev electrons. With a compact design and much higher energy resolution in comparison to previous telescope-type instruments (E.g.  Rept on the van allen probes)  hert will significantly contribute to the quantitative understanding of the radiation belt electron dynamics.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Creating a miniaturized telescope to quantify Earth's energetic particle acceleration.
